judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable substance; Report 2 is slightly more precise on the RESULTS_DB mechanism (quoting the actual default-path code) and more explicit about needing a per-engineer env override beyond just a separate checkout, while Report 1's staging is a bit less formally structured but covers the same reasoning.

report_1.evidence: Group A — pipeline/registry.py (F-02, F-05, F-09): three independent one-line/one-entry config edits (MODEL_ENDPOINTS add, DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION bump, DEFAULT_DATASET path fix) in a tiny file. One engineer, one PR

report_2.evidence: Engineer C: F-02 + F-05 + F-09, all in pipeline/registry.py — bundled to one owner because they're three edits to the same 12-line file; splitting them across engineers would produce three near-simultaneous diffs to adjacent lines for no benefit.

report_1.evidence: Group C — pipeline/rubric.py + tests (F-01, F-11): ... Start second, in parallel with Group B — different file, no dependency between them.

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 (fully parallel, zero file overlap, no product-decision risk — do these first): Engineer A: F-03 ... Engineer B: F-10 ... Engineer C: F-02 + F-05 + F-09 ... Wave 2: Engineer D: F-01 ... plus F-11 ...

report_1.evidence: F-06 needs a threshold decision before it's actionable as an engineering task. ... it should go back to whoever owns the rubric/grading criteria for specific target values first.

report_2.evidence: this needs an explicit decision (specific new threshold values, and sign-off from whoever owns the scoring rubric) before any engineer edits PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS.

report_1.evidence: tests/conftest.py defaults RESULTS_DB to a fixed path, /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db. If multiple engineers run the test suite concurrently on a shared machine/CI runner without overriding RESULTS_DB, they can read/write the same file and produce flaky or corrupted results for each other.

report_2.evidence: tests/conftest.py defines RESULTS_DB = os.environ.get("RESULTS_DB", "/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db") — a single real filesystem path. If engineers working on F-11 and F-12 ... run their suites concurrently ... without overriding RESULTS_DB ... parallel test runs could read/write the same file and produce flaky or corrupted results.
